Job description

Turtle Up Web Design Internship

About Turtle Up
Turtle Up is a registered nonprofit based in Ohio with 501(c)(3) designation. Our mission is to protect Ghana’s sea turtles through sustainable, community-based conservation. We envision a future where marine wildlife and coastal communities thrive together.

We are seeking a Web Design Intern who is highly motivated, organized, and passionate about environmental conservation and social impact. This internship offers a unique opportunity to gain practical experience in nonprofit development, marketing, and website design, while directly supporting conservation work.

Email your resume and cover letter expressing your interest to Corinne Brion at admin@turtleup.org to apply! We will be reviewing applications on a rolling basis and are looking to fill the position ASAP.

Details

Part-time position: 10-15 hours per week preferred, less or more is possible and schedule is flexible.
Unpaid internship with potential for college credit (if institution allows it).
Remote work with scheduled weekly team meetings.
Duration: Semester-based (4-month minimum), with preference for longer commitments.

Responsibilities:

Assist with Turtle Up website (WordPress) maintenance tasks, including content updates, bug fixes, and creation of new webpages and tabs.
Collaborate with social media team to create visually appealing and user-friendly website layouts.
Conduct research on similar sea turtle and conservation non-profit websites to inform best design practices and layouts.
Communicate effectively with team members to ensure timely completion of website updates.

Requirements:

Pursuing a degree in Graphic Design, Web Design, Digital Media, or a related field.
Experience with WordPress required.
Strong visual design skills with a keen eye for detail and aesthetics.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills.
Ability to work independently and manage time effectively in a fast-paced environment.
Enthusiasm for learning and staying updated on design trends and technologies.

To apply, please submit a resume and cover letter detailing your interest in the position and relevant experience to admin@turtleup.org.

Turtle Up is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive work environment and encourages applications from candidates of all backgrounds.

Project Management Roles

Turtle Up aims to sustainably protect and conserve Ghana's sea turtles by embracing a community-based approach. Our vision is to contribute to a future where marine wildlife and people in coastal communities are thriving. 

Turtle Up has a team of over 15 volunteer interns across the U.S. filling diverse positions in fundraising, grant writing, social media, environmental education, and more!

Email admin@turtleup.org to learn more about our open positions and ways to get involved!
